topics: Iran strike debate and containment strategy

claims.claim: Even if Iran restarted its nuclear program after the deal, it would take years to a decade or 15+ years to build enough enriched uranium for a nuclear weapon.
evidence: Restarting would require re-acquiring equipment, rebuilding intelligence infrastructure
Described as a 'natural setback' providing defense for key conditions

claims.claim: The Iran deal at this stage is only at the MOU level of high-level principles and the exact program details have yet to be defined.
evidence: Exact mechanisms for uranium removal still to be defined
Iran gets sanctions relief and normalized US relationship in exchange

claims.claim: J.Cal believes the full-scale war with Iran was not worth it and that a containment/incremental strike approach (like Israel had used) was the superior strategy.
evidence: References Israel's 'trimming of the grass, hedging of the garden approach'
Argues bombing facilities every 18 months was working and did not require full-scale war
States 'we don't know what information Trump had' but still calls the escalation unnecessary

claims.claim: J.Cal argues the US cannot enforce democracy in the Middle East and the Iranian people must lead their own revolution.
evidence: References repeated failures of externally facilitated democracy in the Middle East
States 'the people have to lead their own revolution. We can't facilitate it.'

claims.claim: Allowing North Korea to develop nuclear weapons was a mistake by prior administrations, and the same mistake nearly happened with Iran.
evidence: References a prior discussion with Graham Allison about why North Korea wasn't contained earlier
Argues Iran containment via periodic strikes was working before the full-scale war

quote_candidates.text: it's gonna take years to a decade to 15 years plus to build enough enriched uranium to build a nuclear weapon.
This is at the MOU stage. It's at the level of high-level principles.
we don't see what Trump sees, right? So you have to be humble enough to know we don't know what information he had in all of this
quote_candidates.speaker: J.Cal

quote_candidates.text: Isolating dictators, waiting them out is always the better approach than starting full-scale wars with them, because you don't know what could happen in an escalation.
quote_candidates.speaker: J.Cal

quote_candidates.text: we can't enforce democracy in the Middle East. We've learned that over and over and over again. The people have to take, they have to lead their own revolution.
quote_candidates.speaker: J.Cal

quote_candidates.text: Every 18 months, we bombed the facilities. It was fine. We didn't need to start a full-scale war.
quote_candidates.speaker: J.Cal
